CWR.notes: Trade me shit, I’m fucking bored

content.text: There shall be no fewer than 8 teams and no more than 12 teams
There will an even number of teams at all times
A majority vote of current owners is necessary to add a new owner
If there is a tie, the vote of the commissioner is the tiebreaker
Owners may only be added in pairs, unless they are being added as a co-manager or are replacing a departing owner
A ¾ majority and commissioner approval is needed to remove someone from the league
The owner up for removal does not receive a vote
If the commissioner is the owner whose status is up for vote, they may not use their veto power
Owners are expected to notify the league at least one month prior to the offseason draft if they intend to leave the league.
If someone leaves the league, the league must replace them to comply with Article 1, Section 1/2
The draft will be a snake draft in random order
There will be 20 rounds of 8 picks each
The draft will be held at a date in which all participants can attend, as per a league vote
Picks may be traded, but only for the same number of picks
No future picks may be acquired
Each round will be the reverse order of the standings from the previous season
Teams can pass on utilizing their selection
The offseason draft will be held on Labor Day weekend.
A majority vote will be taken to determine the date and time within that weekend
Changing the weekend of the draft requires a ¾ majority
Picks may be traded up to 4 years in advance, marked by the completion of a draft
The completion of the 2023 offseason draft means picks can be traded in:
Each round will be the reverse order of the standings at the time of the draft
Teams can pass on utilizing their selection
This draft will be held after all week 7 NFL matchups have concluded and before all week 8 matchups
Picks may be traded up to 4 years in advance, marked by the completion of a draft
The completion of the 2023 midseason draft means picks can be traded in:
Each round will be the reverse order of the standings from the previous season
In the Founding Draft, the practice squad draft will be held in the order of the draft, but will not be a snake draft
You may only select one player at any position
Picks may be traded, but only for the same number of picks in return
All teams must hold 4 practice squad picks all all times
Following the conclusion of the practice squad draft, each team may nominate a player to be added to a free agent pool.
Teams do not need to nominate a player
The free agent pool resets at the start of each draft
All players added to the free agent pool must be either
Practice squad players may be activated at any time, but a roster spot must be available or cleared
Players may not be sent down to the practice squad, they may only be released
If a practice squad player is traded, they may be added to the active roster by the team receiving that player or can be left on the practice squad subject to (1.g)
Practice squads can only add players during the annual and midseason draft
Any practice squad player who remains on a practice squad by the Thursday Night game the week of the midseason draft is released to the pool of available players
Any practice squad player who remains on a practice squad by the conclusion of the league championship is released to the pool of available players
There can never be more than one of any position on any team’s practice squad
Trading for other practice squad players does not change this rule
Trades may be made between owners
The trade deadline will be set 4 weeks before the playoffs every season and concludes after the championship is over
Trades may include future picks as allowed in Article 2
Trades do not need to have equal numbers of players or picks
Trades may be rejected by the commissioner to prevent unfair moves
This power may be overridden with a ¾ league vote, in which the commissioner does not receive a vote
All transactions outside of the draft must be announced via email to the league
Transactions all date effect immediately following the email announcement
If a player involved in a transaction has already played, the transaction does not modify lineups for that week but is considered completed.
Additional pieces of the transaction (activations, drops, etc) cannot be modified once the email is sent
Moves should be announced via email to the league
Players must be added directly to active rosters
Active rosters are expected to have:
Each week, a team may start:
Starters must be emailed to the entire league before the beginning of their NFL game
If a team fails to submit a lineup, that team’s owner must submit a video apology to the league while wearing formal attire.
All scoring will be done using ESPN statistics
No fractional points will be awarded
Defensive touchdowns only include pick sixes, fumble returns, blocked punts, and blocked kicks, as well as kick return TDs and punt return TDs
If a coach is started the week preceding his firing, this penalty takes effect for the next week
Owners receive one rank point for a win and/or ½ of a rank point for being in the top half of the league in that week’s scoring
A tie is ½ a rank point
Tying to be the last member in the top half of league scoring is ¼ of a rank point
Standings are calculated by  rank points
If two or more teams are equal in rank points, ties are arbitrated by total number of wins
Further ties are arbitrated by total points scored
Further ties are arbitrated by head to head matchups
If a further tiebreaker is needed, the commissioner may determine a fair one
If any owner involved believes this tiebreaker is unfair, they may ask for a league vote to determine the rule’s fairness, which is approved by a majority vote of all owners
Playoff games cannot tie. The winner advances and the loser is eliminated.
In the event of a tie, the playoff tiebreaker is seed.
The commissioner must collect league fees before the season
The commissioner must collect fines as necessary throughout the season
The commissioner must distribute winnings following the season
The commissioner may veto trades they deem unfair.
The league may override the commissioner by a ¾ vote
The commissioner is responsible for enforcing rules and arbitrating disputes over the rules
The league may override the commissioner by a ¾ vote
The commissioner must update and send the standings to the league via email at the conclusion of every league week
This must be done before the next league week begins
It is encouraged to include a newsletter with the standings
The commissioner is responsible for updating the rosters and traded picks lists when applicable
The commissioner is responsible for setting the date and time of the draft to maximize the number of participants
The draft will be held on Sunday night of Labor Day Weekend unless it becomes necessary to move the draft due to attendance issues.
The commissioner must send scores after each NFL gameday
If a scoring error is made by the commissioner, it may be corrected by any owner before the start of the next league week.
Scoring and rule changes may be modified during the offseason
Any owner may propose a change and bring it to vote in the offseason, with a ⅔ majority needed to change a rule.
The commissioner may veto changes made by ownership vote.
This veto can be overridden by a ¾ majority of owners, including the commissioner
The commissioner may modify scoring/rules in the offseason at will, but if any owner requests a vote on the change, a vote shall be taken with the same rules as 1.g.iii.3
To modify scoring during the season, a vote must be taken in which a ¾ majority of owners vote in favor of the change.
The commissioner may block rule changes during a season.
The commissioner is responsible for maintaining a list of players suspended from the QPFL for external reasons. Players added to the Commissioner’s Exempt List may not be rostered on any team.
In order to be added to the Commissioner’s Exempt List, a vote must be held at an offseason draft following normal voting procedure.
The current Commissioner’s Exempt List is:

articles.title: Article 6: League Fees, Winnings, and Sacko

content.text: Buy in for the league will be $32 dollars
Largest Margin of Victory in Rivalry Week = $20
Any extra money from the loser’s punishment is used for 2.c.ii, any extra money after both 2.c.i and 2.c.ii is given to the winner

The person who must complete the loser’s tournament punishment is decided by a tournament held the same week as the playoffs among the four bottom teams in the standings. Losers “advance” while winners are eliminated safely.

The loser’s tournament punishment is a beer mile, defined as a one mile run in which, before each quarter mile, an entire 12 oz beer must be consumed before the quarter mile begins. Vomiting results in disqualification and a new attempt must be completed. The time will be recorded by the QPFL league mate (or a suitable alternative) who attends the run, and the time will be recorded in the Hall of Fame.
The primary punishment will be given to the loser of the regular season.
This punishment is determined every year before the start of the offseason draft
The sacko commissioner determines if a punishment is completed as per the guidelines of the league.
If the league’s sacko punishment is not completed by the start of the season, that team automatically loses every week until the punishment is completed
The regular season will start for the NFL Week 1
The postseason must conclude by the end of NFL Week 17
The postseason will consist of four teams
The number of weeks of the postseason will be the minimum required to complete a full postseason tournament, as determined by the commissioner
Playoff matches are done by seeding, where the top remaining seed faces the bottom remaining seed
The postseason is single elimination, with the loser of each matchup eliminated
Ties are broken by the regular season standings
